neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Lower Basin
neighborhoods.description: Revitalized downtown along the James River with craft breweries, Community Market, and Bluffwalk trail

housing.predominantType: Converted warehouse lofts and renovated buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Lynchburg. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Forest / Timberlake
neighborhoods.description: Western suburban area with shopping centers, family neighborhoods, and proximity to Smith Mountain Lake
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Lynchburg's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Madison Heights / Amherst
neighborhoods.description: Northern communities across the James River with affordable housing and rural character

housing.predominantType: Modest houses, mobile homes, and affordable apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Boonsboro / Peakland
neighborhoods.description: Established neighborhoods near Liberty University with community parks and good schools

housing.predominantType: Ranch houses, Cape Cods, and traditional homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
